ia_decisions: Pricing kept as a dedicated page — five tiers, three grant overlays, per-tier price_unit semantics; cannot compress into an index slab without losing the funded-procurement narrative.
For-whom kept as one segmented page (three ICPs in one scroll) rather than three persona pages — 8-page site footprint stays reasonable and the segments share a CTA.
FAQ planned on home (compliance/AI/funding flavour, FAQPage JSON-LD-emitting) and on contact (logistics flavour, no JSON-LD to avoid duplicate signals).
About is its own page — Elitez Group's WSG Human Capital Partner / IHRP exclusive partner / SkillsFuture-approved-training-organisation credentials are too load-bearing to bury inside index.
404.html scaffolded (not in nav) — required for any GitHub-Pages multi-page site even though Pages will serve it implicitly.
Admin pages visible in nav per static-informational archetype contract; no password gate initially.
BreadcrumbList JSON-LD planned for every non-home inner page (Agent 6 implementation); home page emits Organization + WebSite + FAQPage instead.

consumes_intel set on home (uses pricing-strategy personas + whitespace attack-plan for hero proof points via Agent 5), product (uses whitespace + pricing for module-library framing), pricing (pricing-strategy + market-intel grants), for-whom (pricing-strategy personas + whitespace heatmap segments), admin (competitors + whitespace), admin-insights (market-intel + pricing + whitespace).
